generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Building Control Systems)
instance OccupancyControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC control valve for occupancy-based room temperature regulation in LEED Platinum academic building"
]

instance LightingDimmerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart LED dimmer for adaptive lighting control in Unreal Engine 5 visualization"
]

instance HVACZoneControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Zone-specific HVAC controller with reinforcement learning integration (BOREALIS project)"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled post-occupancy temperature sensor deployed in building core zones for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental IoT sensor measuring relative humidity with 0.5% accuracy in occupied spaces"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityData
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "PIR-based occupancy detector with 95% detection accuracy for space utilization analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyData
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter aggregating real-time energy consumption data from building sub-systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionData
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Indoor air quality sensor measuring CO₂ levels with 5-minute sampling frequency"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2Data
]

instance LightingEnergySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy-efficient lighting sensor tracking power consumption per zone"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingEnergyData
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance CO2Data : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]

instance LightingEnergyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingEnergySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LEEDPlatinumBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D spatial and functional representation of LEED Platinum academic building components with embedded runtime semantics (geometry, topology, relationships)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMEnrichmentTool
]

instance RuntimeBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model with embedded sensor/actuator metadata and control logic via model-driven transformations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToHonoTransformer
]

instance UnrealEngine5Visualization : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D Unreal Engine 5 environment dynamically updating with DT data for immersive monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineRenderer
]

instance OperationalDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured data repository storing real-time operational metrics from IoT sensors via Eclipse Hono/Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMiddleware
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData, HumidityData, OccupancyData,
                        EnergyConsumptionData, CO2Data, LightingEnergyData
]

instance SimulationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimization parameters for predictive analytics including reinforcement learning models (BOREALIS project)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMEnrichmentTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven tool for embedding sensor metadata and control logic into BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RuntimeBIMModel
]

instance BIMToHonoTransformer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated model-to-model transformation generating Eclipse Hono/Ditto configurations from enriched BIM"
    DTDFVocab:enables HonoMiddleware
]

instance HonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono IoT middleware enabling real-time bidirectional communication between physical sensors/actuators and DT services"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalDataStore
]

instance UnrealEngineRenderer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 visualization engine providing interactive 3D monitoring and simulation capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables UnrealEngine5Visualization
]

instance ReinforcementLearningEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI-driven optimization engine implementing BOREALIS reinforcement learning for adaptive HVAC/lighting control"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveOptimizationService
]

instance CustomServiceExtension : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular extension point for domain-specific plugins (IoT analytics, AI decision support)"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomAnalyticsService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D monitoring via Unreal Engine 5 for interactive building performance assessment"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Energy efficiency and comfort optimization using reinforcement learning (BOREALIS project)"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight, ComfortRecommendationAction
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics plugins for IoT data processing and AI decision support"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomInsight1, CustomInsight2
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated bidirectional communication setup between BIM models and physical assets"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedSetupAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of building performance metrics (temperature, occupancy, energy) in Unreal Engine 5"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Actionable recommendations for energy efficiency improvements based on predictive analytics"
]

instance ComfortRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated HVAC/lighting adjustments to optimize indoor environmental comfort"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AutomatedSetupAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation and deployment of runtime interfaces (Hono/Ditto configurations) from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomInsight1 : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "IoT-specific analytics dashboard for building operational metrics"
]

instance CustomInsight2 : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "AI-driven decision support system for facility management operations"
]

instance PostOccupancyAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Iterative refinement of control policies based on live sensor data for continuous improvement"
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical control signals for automated building system adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment/Component Relationships
instance BuildingCoreEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor,
                  EnergyConsumptionSensor, CO2Sensor
]

instance AcademicBuildingZone : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LightingDimmerActuator, HVACZoneControllerActuator,
                  TemperatureSensor, LightingEnergySensor
]

instance LEEDPlatinumBuilding : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LEEDPlatinumBIMModel, OperationalDataStore,
                  RuntimeBIMModel, UnrealEngine5Visualization
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals and commands generated by DT services are transmitted back to physical building systems via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware**, enabling automated adjustments to BCS (e.g., dynamic lighting control, HVAC optimization). The framework leverages **BIM-based control logic modeling** to define actuator configurations and control strategies, ensuring seamless integration with physical infrastructure. This interaction supports **adaptive lighting systems** (e.g., BOREALIS project) and **energy-efficient operations** through reinforcement learning-driven policies."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates determined by sensor data acquisition frequencies. The framework supports:
  - **Low-latency bidirectional communication** via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, ensuring sub-second updates for critical control signals.
  - **Iterative refinement** of control policies (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) over time, aligning DT behavior with evolving operational data.
  - **Event-driven processing** for asynchronous updates (e.g., occupancy changes, energy spikes), enabling adaptive DT responses."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is a **service-oriented, layered architecture** with the following orchestration:
  - **Core DT layer**: Centralized runtime interface (e.g., Hono/Ditto) managing bidirectional data exchange with physical assets.
  - **BIM integration layer**: Enriched BIM models serve as the semantic foundation for device modeling and control logic definition.
  - **Visualization layer**: Unreal Engine 5 provides interactive 3D representations of the DT, synchronized with real-time data.
  - **Service layer**: Extensible extension points allow DT Engineers to deploy domain-specific plugins (e.g., energy optimization, facility management dashboards).
  - **Control layer**: Real-time synchronization with physical BCS via Hono/Ditto, enabling adaptive operations (e.g., adaptive lighting/HVAC via reinforcement learning)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans all life cycle stages of the building:
  1. **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models include static and dynamic BCS components (e.g., sensor/actuator placements, control logic) for automated DT generation.
  2. **Construction phase**: BIM models are validated and transformed into runtime artifacts (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations) for initial DT deployment.
  3. **Operation phase**: Real-time synchronization with physical assets via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, enabling adaptive control (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) and continuous optimization.
  4. **Refurbishment phase**: DTs support post-occupancy data analysis to inform retrofitting decisions, with modular extensions allowing incremental upgrades."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT fidelity is evaluated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart. Key considerations include:
  - **Semantic coherence**: Automated extraction of BIM metadata ensures consistent representation of physical assets (e.g., sensor placements, control logic).
  - **Real-time synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware minimizes latency in data exchange, reducing semantic drift (as highlighted in Abbasi et al.’s work).
  - **Validation mechanisms**: Model validation steps (e.g., IFC compliance checks) and iterative refinement (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) enhance DT validity.
  - **Uncertainty management**: While not explicitly quantified, the framework assumes that BIM models serve as the primary source of truth, with runtime data (e.g., sensor noise) handled via middleware protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection relies on:
  1. **Network protocols**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** use lightweight, real-time protocols (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) for bidirectional communication between physical sensors/actuators and DT services.
  2. **Middleware architecture**: A layered system coupling BIM models (static) with runtime interfaces (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations), enabling seamless data flow.
  3. **Standardized APIs**: Extension points allow integration with third-party services (e.g., IoT platforms, AI analytics) via RESTful or gRPC interfaces.
  4. **Latency optimization**: Designed for sub-second response times to support real-time control (e.g., adaptive lighting/HVAC)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed via:
  1. **Cloud/edge deployment**: Middleware (Hono/Ditto) can run on edge devices (e.g., IoT gateways) or cloud-based platforms for scalability.
  2. **Unreal Engine 5**: Visualization layer deployed as a standalone application or integrated into the DT orchestration stack.
  3. **Standardized extension points**: Allow modular deployment of custom services (e.g., AI models, IoT plugins) without altering core DT infrastructure.
  4. **BIM-centric workflows**: Deployment begins with BIM model enrichment and automated transformation into runtime artifacts (e.g., Hono/Ditto configurations), reducing manual effort."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT supports **horizontal integration** with external systems via:
  1. **Standardized APIs**: Extension points enable integration with third-party IoT platforms, BIM tools (e.g., IFC), and analytics services (e.g., AI-driven decision support).
  2. **BIM interoperability**: Enriched BIM models serve as a unified semantic foundation for collaboration across AECO stakeholders (e.g., architects, engineers, facility managers).
  3. **Middleware bridges**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto facilitate data exchange with legacy systems or proprietary IoT platforms, ensuring compatibility with heterogeneous infrastructures."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are managed through:
  1. **BIM-centric governance**: Building control engineers define ownership of BIM-enriched data (e.g., sensor metadata, control logic) during deployment.
  2. **Middleware security**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto support encryption (e.g., TLS) and access controls to protect real-time data exchange.
  3. **Compliance frameworks**: Aligns with AECO standards (e.g., IFC) and regulatory mandates (e.g., GDPR) for data privacy.
  4. **Non-functional requirements**: Ensure low-overhead updates and scalable deployment without compromising security."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  1. **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) format for semantic interoperability.
  2. **Digital Twin standards**: Eclipse Ditto and Eclipse Hono follow open-source IoT and DT protocols (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets).
  3. **Model-driven engineering (MDE)**: Automated transformations (model-to-model/model-to-text) for DT generation.
  4. **AECO-specific standards**: Integration with BIM tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) and compliance with industry practices (e.g., LEED certification for the Living Lab case study)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  1. **Encrypted communication**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware uses TLS/SSL for secure data transmission between physical sensors and DT services.
  2. **Access controls**: Standardized extension points enforce role-based permissions (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) to prevent unauthorized access.
  3. **Fault tolerance**: Real-time synchronization via Hono/Ditto ensures resilience to network failures or sensor malfunctions.
  4. **Human safety**: Virtual-to-physical interactions (e.g., control signals) are designed to avoid accidental harm to occupants, with validation steps ensuring compliance with operational safety protocols."
]
